clusters.title: Israel's 10-day ceasefire in Lebanon begins
clusters.neutral_headline: People in Beirut wary of trusting Israel will uphold Lebanon ceasefire
bullet_summary: Lebanon’s residents say they are wary of trusting that Israel will abide by the ceasefire agreement announced by Trump
A 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon took effect Thursday evening, hours after President Trump announced the temporary truce
Displaced Lebanese in Beirut said they do not yet trust whether the ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon will hold
Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Thursday he has agreed to a 10-day ceasefire in Lebanon

items.title: Israel's 10-day ceasefire in Lebanon begins
items.standfirst: The leaders of Lebanon and Israel agreed to begin a 10-day ceasefire starting at 5 p.m. Eastern Time.
items.content: The leaders of Lebanon and Israel agreed to begin a 10-day ceasefire starting at 5 p.m. Eastern Time.

items.title: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire could impact U.S. deal with Iran
items.standfirst: Israel has been fighting the Iranian regime and its proxy Hezbollah in Lebanon across the border. That local ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon could affect U.S. efforts to come to a broader regional agreement with Iran. Charlie D'Agata reports.
items.content: Israel has been fighting the Iranian regime and its proxy Hezbollah in Lebanon across the border. That local ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon could affect U.S. efforts to come to a broader regional agreement with Iran. Charlie D'Agata reports.

items.title: People in Beirut wary of trusting Israel will uphold Lebanon ceasefire
items.standfirst: Lebanon’s residents say they are wary of trusting that Israel will abide by the ceasefire agreement announced by Trump.
items.content: Lebanon’s residents say they are wary of trusting that Israel will abide by the ceasefire agreement announced by Trump.

items.title: Israel and Lebanon’s 10-day ceasefire goes into effect
items.standfirst: A 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon has gone into effect. It began at midnight local time (21:00 GMT).
items.content: A 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon has gone into effect. It began at midnight local time (21:00 GMT).

items.title: Displaced Lebanese wary as ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah begins
items.standfirst: Tensions linger as evacuees await clarity on the ceasefire’s terms before risking a return to war-torn villages.
items.content: Tensions linger as evacuees await clarity on the ceasefire’s terms before risking a return to war-torn villages.

items.title: Israel agrees to halt its war with Hizbollah in Lebanon
items.standfirst: Ceasefire could increase the chance of securing a permanent end to conflict between the US and Iran
items.content: Ceasefire could increase the chance of securing a permanent end to conflict between the US and Iran

items.title: 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire takes effect
items.standfirst: A 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon took effect Thursday evening, hours after President Trump announced the temporary truce. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata and Joe Zacks have the latest on the state of conflict in the Middle East.
items.content: A 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon took effect Thursday evening, hours after President Trump announced the temporary truce. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata and Joe Zacks have the latest on the state of conflict in the Middle East.

items.title: Israel and Lebanon agree 10-day ceasefire, Trump says
items.standfirst: The US president invites the two countries' leaders to Washington as Israel's prime minister insists troops will not leave southern Lebanon.
items.content: The US president invites the two countries' leaders to Washington as Israel's prime minister insists troops will not leave southern Lebanon.

items.title: Displaced Lebanese in Beirut uncertain if they can trust ceasefire
items.standfirst: Displaced Lebanese in Beirut said they do not yet trust whether the ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon will hold.
items.content: Displaced Lebanese in Beirut said they do not yet trust whether the ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon will hold.

items.title: Israel's Netanyahu says he agrees to a 10-day ceasefire in Lebanon
items.standfirst: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Thursday he has agreed to a 10-day ceasefire in Lebanon. In a video statement, Netanyahu said he was taking the step in an attempt “to advance” peace efforts with Lebanon. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports from Washington.
items.content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Thursday he has agreed to a 10-day ceasefire in Lebanon. In a video statement, Netanyahu said he was taking the step in an attempt “to advance” peace efforts with Lebanon. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports from Washington.

items.title: Live: Israel’s Netanyahu confirms he agreed to 10-day ceasefire with Lebanon
A complex set of negotiations to end Israel's overlapping wars
items.standfirst: Israel has agreed to a ceasefire in Lebanon, part of a complex web of Mideast negotiations, from Iran to Gaza.
items.content: Israel has agreed to a ceasefire in Lebanon, part of a complex web of Mideast negotiations, from Iran to Gaza.(Image credit: Hussein Malla)

items.title: Israel and Lebanon agree to 10-day ceasefire
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump announced that the leaders of Israel and Lebanon had reached a ceasefire deal as fighting raged between Israel and the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah.
items.content: President Donald Trump announced that the leaders of Israel and Lebanon had reached a ceasefire deal as fighting raged between Israel and the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah.

items.title: Lebanon president had phone call with Trump, thanked him for 'efforts' towards ceasefire
items.standfirst: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun held a phone call with Donald Trump on Thursday during which he thanked the US leader for his "efforts" to secure a ceasefire with Israel, the presidency in Beirut said. The call comes after Aoun rejected a US request for a "direct call" with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, according to an official Lebanese source, and a day after Trump announced an expected call between the two countries' "leaders". FRANCE 24's Cyril Payen reports from Beirut, Lebanon.
items.content: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun held a phone call with Donald Trump on Thursday during which he thanked the US leader for his "efforts" to secure a ceasefire with Israel, the presidency in Beirut said. The call comes after Aoun rejected a US request for a "direct call" with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, according to an official Lebanese source, and a day after Trump announced an expected call between the two countries' "leaders". FRANCE 24's Cyril Payen reports from Beirut, Lebanon.

clusters.title: South African opposition leader Malema gets jail sentence
clusters.neutral_headline: South African politician Julius Malema given five-year jail term for gun offence
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, BBC World, Guardian World and others

items.title: South African opposition leader Malema gets jail sentence
items.standfirst: In tonight's edition, South Africa’s firebrand, leftist leader Julius Malema is sentenced to five years in prison convicted of firearm offences. Also, on a visit to Cameroon, Pope Leo condemns so-called "tyrants" driving global wars and the exploitation of Africa’s resources. And we’ll have a look at a new online database shaking up the restitution debate.
items.content: In tonight's edition, South Africa’s firebrand, leftist leader Julius Malema is sentenced to five years in prison convicted of firearm offences. Also, on a visit to Cameroon, Pope Leo condemns so-called "tyrants" driving global wars and the exploitation of Africa’s resources. And we’ll have a look at a new online database shaking up the restitution debate.

items.title: South African opposition figure Malema sentenced to five years in prison for firing a gun
items.standfirst: Malema is appealing against the decision, which means he is not going to prison immediately.
items.content: Malema is appealing against the decision, which means he is not going to prison immediately.

items.title: South African politician Julius Malema given five-year jail term for gun offence
items.standfirst: Leader of leftwing Economic Freedom Fighters was convicted last year for firing rifle in the air at 2018 rallyThe South African leftwing politician Julius Malema has been sentenced to five years in prison for firing a rifle in the air at a political rally in 2018.Lawyers for the leader of the Economic Freedom Fighters, South Africa’s fourth largest political party, immediately appealed, and Malema will remain free while the appeal proceedings are under way. Continue reading...
items.content: Leader of leftwing Economic Freedom Fighters was convicted last year for firing rifle in the air at 2018 rallyThe South African leftwing politician Julius Malema has been sentenced to five years in prison for firing a rifle in the air at a political rally in 2018.Lawyers for the leader of the Economic Freedom Fighters, South Africa’s fourth largest political party, immediately appealed, and Malema will remain free while the appeal proceedings are under way. Continue reading...

items.title: South African opposition leader jailed for firing rifle at rally
items.standfirst: A South African opposition leader has been handed a five-year prison sentence for firing a rifle in the air at a rally.
items.content: A South African opposition leader has been handed a five-year prison sentence for firing a rifle in the air at a rally.

items.content: It comes after it emerged the peer failed security vetting but the Foreign Office still allowed him to take up the post.

items.title: Foreign Office’s top civil servant Olly Robbins forced out over Mandelson vetting row
items.standfirst: Keir Starmer understood to have lost confidence in official over decision to override security vetting failureMandelson failed vetting but Foreign Office overruled decisionSir Olly Robbins, the UK Foreign Office’s top civil servant, has been forced out of his post after the decision to fail Peter Mandelson during his security vetting was overruled by his department.Robbins was the Foreign Office’s most senior official in late January 2025 when the decision was made, paving the way for Mandelson to become the US ambassador. Continue reading...
items.content: Keir Starmer understood to have lost confidence in official over decision to override security vetting failureMandelson failed vetting but Foreign Office overruled decisionSir Olly Robbins, the UK Foreign Office’s top civil servant, has been forced out of his post after the decision to fail Peter Mandelson during his security vetting was overruled by his department.Robbins was the Foreign Office’s most senior official in late January 2025 when the decision was made, paving the way for Mandelson to become the US ambassador. Continue reading...

items.title: UK’s Starmer under fire over report Mandelson failed security vetting
items.standfirst: Gov's spokesperson says Starmer had no knowledge of the Foreign Office overriding the security recommendation.
items.content: Gov's spokesperson says Starmer had no knowledge of the Foreign Office overriding the security recommendation.

items.standfirst: Starmer did not know Mandelson failed vetting, government says

clusters.title: Former Virginia Lt. Gov. Justin Fairfax kills wife, fatally shoots self, police say
clusters.neutral_headline: Ex-Virginia deputy governor kills wife and himself in murder-suicide, police say
bullet_summary: Justin Fairfax shot and killed his wife and then turned the gun on himself what police described as a murder-suicide in their home in Annandale, Virginia, police said Thursday
Reported by CBS News, BBC World

items.title: Former Virginia Lt. Gov. Justin Fairfax kills wife, fatally shoots self, police say
items.standfirst: Former Virginia Lt. Gov. Justin Fairfax shot and killed his wife and then turned the gun on himself what police described as a murder-suicide in their home in Annandale, Virginia, police said Thursday.
items.content: Former Virginia Lt. Gov. Justin Fairfax shot and killed his wife and then turned the gun on himself what police described as a murder-suicide in their home in Annandale, Virginia, police said Thursday.

items.title: Police say Virginia politician killed his wife and himself with teenagers home
items.standfirst: Police in Virginia say the former Lt. Gov. Justin Fairfax died after he shot and killed his wife and then turned the gun on himself. Nicole Sganga reports.
items.content: Police in Virginia say the former Lt. Gov. Justin Fairfax died after he shot and killed his wife and then turned the gun on himself. Nicole Sganga reports.

items.title: Justin Fairfax, wife were going through contentious divorce before murder-suicide, police say
items.standfirst: Just after midnight on Thursday, police say former Virginia Lt. Gov. Justin Fairfax killed his wife and then himself in their home. Both of their teenage children were home at the time of the incident. CBS News homeland security correspondent Nicole Sganga reports.
items.content: Just after midnight on Thursday, police say former Virginia Lt. Gov. Justin Fairfax killed his wife and then himself in their home. Both of their teenage children were home at the time of the incident. CBS News homeland security correspondent Nicole Sganga reports.

items.title: Ex-Virginia deputy governor kills wife and himself in murder-suicide, police say
items.standfirst: Investigators say Justin Fairfax shot his wife, Cerina, multiple times before turning the gun on himself.
items.content: Investigators say Justin Fairfax shot his wife, Cerina, multiple times before turning the gun on himself.

items.title: RFK Jr accused of ‘dangerous conspiracy theories’ at heated budget hearing
items.standfirst: Trump ally grilled by lawmakers over ‘terrible decisions’ on vaccines, public health and funding cuts to key programsUS politics – latest updatesVaccines and public health dominated a frequently contentious hearing with Robert F Kennedy Jr on Thursday before the US House ways and means committee.Kennedy, the health secretary and a longtime vaccine opponent, has overseen sweeping changes to routine vaccination recommendations and has promoted misinformation even amid the biggest measles outbreak in decades. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Trump nominates Erica Schwartz, ex-deputy surgeon general, as CDC director
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump nominates Erica Schwartz, ex-deputy surgeon general, as CDC director
bullet_summary: Trump revealed his choice on Truth Social, saying: “I am pleased to announce the new leadership of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC)
Reported by Guardian World, NPR News

items.title: Trump nominates Erica Schwartz, ex-deputy surgeon general, as CDC director
items.standfirst: Schwartz was deputy surgeon general under Trump’s first administration and is a rear admiral in the US Coast GuardDonald Trump has selected Erica Schwartz to lead the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), bringing to an end a months-long search for a permanent head of the troubled public health agency.Trump revealed his choice on Truth Social, saying: “I am pleased to announce the new leadership of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC). It is my Honor to nominate the incredibly talented Dr Erica Schwartz, MD, JD, MPH, as my Director of the CDC,” he wrote. “She is a STAR!” Continue reading...
items.content: Schwartz was deputy surgeon general under Trump’s first administration and is a rear admiral in the US Coast GuardDonald Trump has selected Erica Schwartz to lead the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), bringing to an end a months-long search for a permanent head of the troubled public health agency.Trump revealed his choice on Truth Social, saying: “I am pleased to announce the new leadership of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC). It is my Honor to nominate the incredibly talented Dr Erica Schwartz, MD, JD, MPH, as my Director of the CDC,” he wrote. “She is a STAR!” Continue reading...

items.title: Trump nominates former Coast Guard doctor as CDC chief
items.standfirst: The nomination comes after months of interim leadership at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.
items.content: The nomination comes after months of interim leadership at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. (Image credit: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services)

items.title: House breaks with Trump, votes against ending deportation protections for Haitians

items.content: All House Democrats and four Republicans forced a vote on a measure to stop the Trump administration from ending temporary deportation protections for more than 300,000 people from Haiti.

clusters.title: Senate Democrats move to stall Trump’s ‘absurd’ bid to install new Fed chair
clusters.neutral_headline: Senate Democrats move to stall Trump’s ‘absurd’ bid to install new Fed chair
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, Guardian Business

items.title: Senate Democrats move to stall Trump’s ‘absurd’ bid to install new Fed chair
items.standfirst: Democratic lawmakers urged Republican leaders to postpone the confirmation hearing of Kevin WarshSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxDemocrats have moved to stall Donald Trump’s effort to exert greater control over the US Federal Reserve, condemning the president’s “absurd” bid to install a new leader of the central bank while it is targeted with criminal investigations.Democratic lawmakers on the Senate banking committee urged its Republican leadership on Thursday to postpone the planned confirmation hearing for Kevin Warsh, the financial executive and former Fed governor Trump has nominated to replace Jerome Powell as Fed chair. Continue reading...
items.content: Democratic lawmakers urged Republican leaders to postpone the confirmation hearing of Kevin WarshSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxDemocrats have moved to stall Donald Trump’s effort to exert greater control over the US Federal Reserve, condemning the president’s “absurd” bid to install a new leader of the central bank while it is targeted with criminal investigations.Democratic lawmakers on the Senate banking committee urged its Republican leadership on Thursday to postpone the planned confirmation hearing for Kevin Warsh, the financial executive and former Fed governor Trump has nominated to replace Jerome Powell as Fed chair. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump threatens to fire Fed chair Jerome Powell amid pressure campaign
items.standfirst: US president says he has ‘held back’ on firing the head of the Federal Reserve leading up to end of Powell’s term in MaySign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxDonald Trump threatened to fire Jerome Powell if he stays on as US Federal Reserve chair past the end of his tenure and doubled down on a criminal investigation into renovations of the central bank’s headquarters.As the White House pushes Trump’s new nominee to take charge of the Fed, Kevin Warsh, Powell has a month left in the role. The possibility of Powell staying on as chair past 15 May, the official end of his term, has grown amid mounting scrutiny of Trump’s approach to the Fed in the Senate, which is required to approve Warsh’s nomination. Continue reading...
items.content: US president says he has ‘held back’ on firing the head of the Federal Reserve leading up to end of Powell’s term in MaySign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxDonald Trump threatened to fire Jerome Powell if he stays on as US Federal Reserve chair past the end of his tenure and doubled down on a criminal investigation into renovations of the central bank’s headquarters.As the White House pushes Trump’s new nominee to take charge of the Fed, Kevin Warsh, Powell has a month left in the role. The possibility of Powell staying on as chair past 15 May, the official end of his term, has grown amid mounting scrutiny of Trump’s approach to the Fed in the Senate, which is required to approve Warsh’s nomination. Continue reading...

items.title: Armed robbers hold 25 people hostage at Naples bank before fleeing through hole in floor
items.standfirst: Thieves believed to have escaped into sewers after holding staff and customers in Crédit Agricole branch for two hoursArmed robbers held 25 people hostage at a bank in Naples for two hours on Thursday, before fleeing through a tunnel.The three thieves entered a branch of Crédit Agricole in the southern Italian city at about 11.30am, taking hostage staff and customers, who were freed by police a couple of hours later. items.title: UK prepares for food shortages in worst case scenario as Iran war continues
items.standfirst: The UK could face some food shortages by the summer under a worst case scenario drawn up by government officials.
items.content: The UK could face some food shortages by the summer under a worst case scenario drawn up by government officials.

items.title: UK could face gaps on supermarket shelves by summer if Iran war continues
items.standfirst: Ministers drawing up contingency plans for ‘reasonable worst-case scenario’ if supply of CO2 is disruptedBusiness live – latest updatesThe UK could face some gaps on supermarket shelves this summer if disruption caused by the Iran war continues, with shortages of carbon dioxide potentially hitting supplies of chicken, pork and fizzy drinks.Government ministers are drawing up contingency plans for a “reasonable worst-case scenario” if the key shipping lane of the strait of Hormuz does not reopen, disrupting supplies of the CO2 required by the food industry. Continue reading...
